notes: Shot order mirrors the kShots[] table in main.cpp. Each full-frame shot produces one screenshot_NNNNNN.png in order — the Nth entry here maps to the Nth full-frame screenshot by numeric index. This 'shots' list enumerates only the full-frame shots (render-verify's _collect_shots keeps crop PNGs out of the index mapping). Reference PNGs live in sibling subdirs named after the CMake preset (e.g. macos-debug/, linux-debug/) since GL and Metal produce pixel-different output. OPTIONAL GATE BLOCKS (epic #1766 T-2; both additive — omit for the original full-frame-only behavior): 'crops' = {shotLabel: [cropLabel, ...]} pixel-diffs each declared ROI crop (captured screenshot_NNNNNN_<shotLabel>__crop_<cropLabel>.png) against a committed reference crop named <shotLabel>__crop_<cropLabel>.png in the backend dir, using the same 'thresholds'; crops not listed here stay inspection-only. 'structural' = {shotLabel: [{metric, roi?, <threshold>...}]} runs the backend-agnostic render-<metric>-metric.py gate on that shot's full-frame capture — e.g. {metric: 'shadow', roi: [x,y,w,h], max_hole_ratio: 0.05, max_components: 8} invokes render-shadow-metric.py (#1765) with --roi/--max-hole-ratio/--max-components and fails the run on a non-zero exit. Threshold keys map to CLI flags by key.replace('_','-'); each structural gate needs at least one threshold key. 'shadow' is the only metric implemented today (coverage/silhouette/clip land in T-3); referencing an unimplemented metric fails loudly. The shape_debug crops (zoom4_origin / zoom8_origin / zoom8_yaw180) are not gated yet — they have no committed reference crops; opt them into 'crops' once references are captured via --update-references.
